{{Quote|Your family is dead, boy! Come with us or die here, too!|[[Jaster Mereel]]|<ref name="Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1">[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]]</ref>}}
The '''Battle of Concord Dawn''' was a battle of the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]].

==The battle==
The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] splinter group [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], led by [[Tor Vizsla|Vizsla]], attacked the planet [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], which held a large population of Mandalorians loyal to the "[[True Mandalorians|True Mandalorian]]" faction led by [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Jaster Mereel]], who had been born on Concord Dawn. The True Mandalorians scattered, and the Death Watch spent several days tracking them down.

The [[Fett (Elder Journeyman Protector)|man]] who had succeeded Mereel's position of [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]] following his exile, hid Mereel and his forces at [[Fett homestead|his homestead]]. As such, the homestead was destroyed, and the Protector killed by the Death Watch—along with his wife. Only his two children, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango]] and [[Arla Fett]], survived. Jango helped Mereel defeat the Death Watch by leading him out of the homestead and taking him to the nearest town. Arla, later on, was rescued and put into a mental institution on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

After believing himself to have won the battle, Vizsla planned to next attack [[Moonus Mandel/Legends|Moonus Mandel]], but would first recuperate and stock up on supplies. When the Death Watch entered the town, the Mandalorians ambushed them, and destroyed their armored vehicle. Fett personally set the charge and blasted a high-ranking Death Watch member. However, Vizsla managed to escape in the end.

==Aftermath==
The young Jango was taken under the protection of Mereel, and joined his group of Mandalorians. For six years, the True Mandalorians believed this to be the end of the Death Watch.
